content.full_text:
McCUTCHEN, DOYLE, BROWN & ENERSEN
COUNSELORS AT LAW
1331 NORTH CALIFORNIA BOULEVARD
POST OFFICE BOX V
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA 94596
TELEPHONE (510) 937-8000
FACSIMILE (510) 975-5390

SAN FRANCISCO
LOS ANGELES
SAN JOSE
WALNUT CREEK

WASHINGTON, D.C
TAIPEI
AFFILIATED OFFICE
BANGKOK

April 13, 1992

DIRECT DIAL NUMBER
(510) 975-5318

Sandra Taylor
Political Reform Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95816

Dear Ms. Taylor:

This letter serves to confirm your recent informal telephone advice.

(1) The costs incurred before an initiative is in circulation are not reportable events. "Circulation" means just that, circulation, not publication of intent to circulate. These costs include the attorneys' fees incurred when preparing a draft of the initiative, political consulting voter polling about the proposed text. Delivery of the attorney-prepared initiative to a Qualification Committee does not require reporting the attorney's time. But, if a pre-circulation poll is given to the Qualification Committee for use in its qualification effort, the cost of preparing the poll would then be a reportable event.

(2) If a consultant is hired on a basis of "payment only if successful," the fair market value of the consultant's time is reportable as an in-kind loan before the election.

Very truly yours,

Palmer Brown Madden

PBM:tn/3
0862G

MICROFILM DUPLICATE RECORD IN USE
[illegible]
[illegible]

[The rest of the document appears to be blank or too faint to read clearly]
